one_line_reason: Well-built and fast-growing, but growth is 100% SEO/content (the team's weakness — the growth-lever gate fails → PASS) in the most AI-Overview-exposed niche, with unproven week-old AdSense revenue and classic build-to-flip timing at the top of a 3-month ramp.

operability_upside.note: Trivial to run (calculators are ~10 lines of Watt's-law JS), but near-zero engineering/product upside to unlock — a mature commodity where software skill adds almost nothing; the growth-need lives in the gate, not here.

pmf_retention.note: Real underlying search demand, but zero user loyalty — pure Google organic pass-through traffic with no return/branded behavior.

unit_economics.note: AdSense approved ~1 week ago with no earnings shared, so no real revenue history; display-only, international/low-CPC, informational queries all imply structurally low RPM.

moat_defensibility.note: Commodity in a crowded field with a single Google-organic channel; Google's own answer box plus rapidtables/inchcalculator already rank — nothing to defend.

growth_market.note: Impressions ramping now, but the unit-conversion niche is the most AI-Overview / instant-answer exposed category (0.6% CTR already shows in-SERP disintermediation), so the category is being eaten.

tech_ip.note: Site itself is simple and transfers cleanly, but content provenance is unknown (AI-generated? 18 auto-translations?) and any prior manual penalties/ownership are unverified.

transfer_key_person.note: Growth engine is impersonal SEO/domain that survives founder exit, but AdSense does not transfer (new owner re-applies → day-one monetization gap) and the content-generation process transfer is unproven.

deal_terms.note: No asking price or multiple basis provided — cannot assess value-for-money or absolute affordability.

seller_adverse_selection.note: Brand-new site monetized just last week and already listed at the top of a 3-month ramp — classic build-to-flip, strong adverse-selection signal.

key_unknowns: Actual AdSense earnings and page RPM since approval (only days old) — swings unit economics; near-zero would confirm 'not a business yet'.
Asking price and its basis — needed for deal terms/affordability, though it does not change the PASS given growth_lever_fits=false.
Query/page mix and AI-Overview presence on the head 'watts to volts' term vs the blog — how much of the ~264k monthly impressions is being disintermediated in-SERP.
Content provenance and IP ownership (AI-generated? 18 auto-translations?), and any prior Google manual penalties.
Traffic composition: 100% Google organic vs any branded/direct.
seller_questions: Screen-share the AdSense dashboard: actual daily earnings and page RPM since approval. (unit economics; also: why sell one week after monetizing?)
Why sell now, one week after monetizing? How many sites have you built and sold? (adverse selection)

Search Console by query and by page: share of clicks from the 'watts to volts' head term vs the blog; do top queries show an AI Overview? Trend through the last core update? (fragility / growth-market)
Asking price and its basis — a multiple of what profit? (deal terms)
Is the content and are the 18 language versions AI-generated? Who owns the IP? Any manual penalties? What % of traffic is branded/direct vs Google organic? (IP + fragility)
What transfers — domain, GA/GSC, social handles, email? Note AdSense does not transfer; the new owner re-applies, so plan a day-one monetization gap. (transfer / key-person)

AdSense approved 5-10 days ago [ASSUMED — user-stated, unverified]

sources.title: Forbes — Google AI Overviews are eating your traffic (2026)

scorecard_markdown:
## Acquisition Scorecard v2 — wattstovolts.com

**Verdict: PASS — merit 34/100 (completeness ~56%).** Content/calculator site (AdSense/display-ad), ~3 months old, no asking price. Capped at PASS by the `growth_lever_fits=false` kill gate: the #1 acquisition channel is a Google-organic SEO/content treadmill — precisely this team's weakness — in the niche most exposed to AI-Overview disintermediation, with unproven week-old AdSense revenue and build-to-flip timing.

### Dimensions (score/5, confidence)
- **Operability & product upside:** 2 (high) — trivial to run (~10 lines of JS), near-zero engineering/product upside.
- **PMF & retention:** 2 (med) — real search demand, zero loyalty, pure Google pass-through.
- **Unit economics:** 2 (low) — AdSense ~1wk old, no earnings shared; display/international/informational → low RPM.
- **Moat & defensibility:** 1 (high) — commodity, single organic channel; Google's answer box + rapidtables rank.
- **Growth & market:** 1 (med) — ramping now but category being eaten by AI Overviews / instant answers (0.6% CTR).
- **Tech & IP health:** 2 (low) — simple/transferable site, but content provenance and penalty history unknown.
- **Transfer / key-person:** 2 (low) — SEO/domain is impersonal and transfers, but AdSense re-apply = day-one gap.
- **Deal terms & value:** 2 (unknown) — no asking price provided.
- **Seller & adverse-selection:** 1 (med) — monetized last week, already for sale at top of a 3-month ramp; build-to-flip.

### Gates
- operable: **true** · growth_lever_fits: **FALSE (kill → PASS)** · ip_transferable: **unknown (provisional)** · value_transfers: **true** · not_fragile: **false** (structural: 100% Google-organic access, undefendable + AI-disintermediated) · revenue_evidence: **none** (no verified earnings; provisional).
- Affordability: asking price null and merit is PASS (<45), so no OUT-OF-BUDGET override — flag suppressed.

### Key unknowns
Actual AdSense RPM/earnings · asking price & basis · AI-Overview exposure of head terms · content provenance/IP ownership & penalties · branded vs 100% organic traffic mix.
